course.title: Lesson 89: A Slip of the Tongue

segments.text: First listen, and then answer the question.

translations.zh-Hans: 先听录音，然后回答问题。

segments.text: Who made the only funny joke that evening and why?

translations.zh-Hans: 那天晚上是谁说了唯一一个好笑的笑话？为什么？

segments.text: People will do anything to see a free show, even if it is a bad one.

translations.zh-Hans: 为了看免费演出，人们什么都愿意做，哪怕演出很糟糕。

segments.text: When the news got round that a comedy show would be presented at our local cinema by the P. and U. Bird Seed Company, we all rushed to see it.

translations.zh-Hans: 听说P&U鸟食公司要在本地电影院举办一场喜剧演出后，我们全都赶去观看。

segments.text: We had to queue for hours to get in, and there must have been several hundred people present just before the show began.

translations.zh-Hans: 为了入场，我们不得不排队数小时；演出开始前，在场的一定有好几百人。

segments.text: Unfortunately, the show was one of the dullest we have ever seen.

translations.zh-Hans: 不幸的是，这是我们看过的最乏味的演出之一。

segments.text: Those who failed to get in need not have felt disappointed, as many of the artists who should have appeared did not come.

translations.zh-Hans: 那些没能入场的人其实不必失望，因为许多本应登台的演员都没有来。

segments.text: The only funny things we heard that evening came from the advertiser at the beginning of the program.

translations.zh-Hans: 那天晚上，我们听到的唯一笑料来自节目开始时的报幕员。

segments.text: He was obviously very nervous, and for some minutes stood awkwardly before the microphone.

translations.zh-Hans: 他显然非常紧张，在麦克风前局促地站了好几分钟。

segments.text: As soon as he opened his mouth, everyone burst out laughing.

translations.zh-Hans: 他一开口，所有人便哄堂大笑。

segments.text: We all know what the poor man should have said, but what he actually said was, This is the Poo and Ee.

translations.zh-Hans: 我们都知道这个可怜的人本来应该说什么，但他实际说的却是：“这里是Poo and Ee”
